query: we're open-sourcing our deployment scripts and i need to scrub them before pushing to the public repo. here's the main deploy.sh — it's got our internal docker registry url, the prod cluster name, and a hardcoded db password that someone left in from 2021

sharing this k8s deployment yaml with an outside auditor, need to remove anything that identifies our infrastructure:

apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: payments-api
  namespace: acme-prod
spec:
  template:
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: payments-api
        image: registry.acme.internal/payments-api:v2.3.1
        env:
        - name: DB_HOST
          value: payments-primary.rds.acme.internal
can you anonymize this? it's a github actions workflow we use for releases, going to include it in a blog post but it has our aws account id baked into the ecr url and our internal slack webhook
hey quick q — is this dockerfile safe to share publicly? it references our internal base image registry and there might be some build args with credentials

got an ansible playbook i need to post on stack overflow to ask about the task ordering logic. obviously need to strip out our inventory hostnames and vault passwords before i paste it. can you do that
remove all the internal stuff from this runbook so i can add it to our public docs. it mentions incident INC-2024-0887, our pagerduty routing key, and a few internal dashboard urls

we're contributing this monitoring script to an open source project, but first need to strip out: our aws account id (shows up in a few cloudwatch arns), our company's internal domain (metrics.corp.acme.io), and a slack webhook token
sanitize this ci/cd config for me — i want to share it with the devops community on reddit but it has our internal artifact repo url and some project names that identify the team

i'm writing up a postmortem template for our team wiki and want to include a real example from last month's outage, but obviously redact the internal bits — server names, account numbers, team names. here's the raw notes
can you review this terraform config for security issues? i want to make sure we're following least privilege and that none of the iam policies are too permissive
how should i store secrets in a kubernetes cluster? we're currently using env vars directly in the deployment yaml and i know that's not ideal
add secret scanning to this github actions workflow so it fails if anyone accidentally commits credentials
we need to rotate our aws access keys — can you help me write a script to automate the rotation process and update the secrets in parameter store
rewrite this bash deploy script in python. same logic, just want to move away from bash for better error handling
update this github actions workflow to use node 20 instead of node 16, the old version is getting deprecated
encrypt this config file with ansible vault so we can check it into git safely
is there a way to audit who has access to our aws account? we think some old iam roles from a contractor last year might still be active
this terraform plan output is showing some unexpected changes to our security groups — can you help me understand what's going on and whether it's safe to apply
generate a github actions workflow that builds and pushes a docker image to ecr on every push to main. we're using node 18 and the ecr repo already exists
